purpose: Enumerate the 33 RED positive tests at HEAD 671a984 and identify, for each, the missing AST source/sink pattern in canonical taint banks. Reframe scope from M3-CF-01's 'source-bank coverage gap' framing to the empirically observed mix of source AND sink gaps.
discrepancy_note: M3-CF-01 documents 35 RED at M3; M4 closed 2 Python tests (path_traversal, deserialization); M4-CF-01 documents 1 remaining (python_xss_positive). Net at HEAD = 35 - 2 = 33. The dispatch task header in the orchestrator brief says 32 (off-by-one — likely counted 'after Python xss is acknowledged as a Python sink-bank gap' which is the same class). Authoritative count from cargo test: 33 RED.

scope_reframe.original_framing: vuln.rs's deleted get_sources had per-language source patterns; the canonical AST source banks lack patterns that get_sources had per-vuln-type
scope_reframe.actual_finding: vuln.rs get_sources was ONLY per-language (NOT per-vuln-type). Sources have 5 small per-language gaps. Sinks (get_sinks WAS per-(vuln_type, language)) have ~25 gaps that the M2 sink-audit did not catch because M2 audited only one shape per (lang, vuln_type) pair against canonical AST sinks; the test fixtures exercise *additional* shapes that are out of M2's audit set. The naming 'source-parity-v1' is preserved for continuity with the carry-forward ID, but the milestone scope IS source-AND-sink parity.
scope_reframe.evidence: Empirical run of all 33 fixtures against the post-M4 binary at HEAD 671a984 — 27 of 33 produce zero findings (no flow constructed; missing source OR sink); 6 produce findings of the WRONG vuln_type (engine constructs a flow but classifies it as the wrong VulnType because the matching sink uses the 'closest existing TaintSinkType' instead of the semantically-correct one — e.g., res.send wired as FileWrite gets classified as PathTraversal but the test asserts Xss).

language_scope.scope_decision: EXPAND beyond the 6 spec-named languages. Excluding rust/cpp/c/swift/ocaml/ruby/javascript/typescript/python/luau would leave 21 of 33 RED tests un-fixed and the milestone goal ('166 GREEN after the milestone') unmet. Spec-named-language framing reflected M3's partial-restoration audit boundary; the empirical coverage gap is broader. The plan addresses ALL 33.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — C_AST_SINKS has NO SqlQuery variant. C-language SQL libraries (libmysqlclient, libpq) absent from sink bank. Pre-M3 vuln.rs get_sinks for (SqlInjection, C) was vec![("mysql_query", "MySQL C client query")]
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: C_AST_SINKS: AstSinkPattern { call_names: &["mysql_query", "PQexec", "sqlite3_exec"], member_patterns: &[], sink_type: TaintSinkType::SqlQuery }

red_tests_full_audit.source_status_in_canonical: PRESENT — CPP_AST_SOURCES line 2514 call_names: ["getenv"] (matches both bare getenv and std::getenv via tree-sitter's qualified-identifier text)
red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — CPP_AST_SINKS has NO SqlQuery variant
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: CPP_AST_SINKS: AstSinkPattern { call_names: &["mysql_query", "PQexec", "sqlite3_exec"], member_patterns: &[("", "std::stoi")], sink_type: TaintSinkType::SqlQuery } — call_names variant matches both bare and qualified

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PRESENT — CPP_AST_SINKS line 2533 ShellExec includes ("", "std::system")
red_tests_full_audit.diagnosis: Both source AND sink are present in canonical banks; flow MUST be failing for a downstream reason. RUNTIME EVIDENCE: zero findings. ROOT CAUSE: tree-sitter-cpp's parse for `std::getenv("CMD")` produces a call_expression whose function-field is qualified_identifier; extract_call_name_c returns 'std::getenv'. The C_AST_SOURCES uses call_names: ["getenv"]; for an exact-match call_names path, 'std::getenv' != 'getenv'. CPP path uses CPP_AST_SOURCES: call_names: ["getenv"] — same exact-match; misses 'std::getenv'.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: CPP_AST_SOURCES — extend EnvVar entry to also accept the qualified form. Two options: (a) add 'std::getenv' to call_names; (b) add member_patterns: &[("", "std::getenv")] for raw fallback. Option (a) is preferred (mirrors how std::system is handled in CPP_AST_SINKS).

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — CPP_AST_SINKS line 2546 FileOpen includes call_names: ["fopen"] but NOT 'std::fopen'. tree-sitter-cpp parses std::fopen(p,...) as a call whose function is qualified_identifier 'std::fopen' — exact-match call_names misses it.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition_a: Source — same fix as cpp_command_injection_positive (add std::getenv).
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition_b: CPP_AST_SINKS FileOpen — extend call_names to include 'std::fopen', 'std::freopen', 'std::ofstream' (the std-qualified C-stdio bridges). Same shape as the existing 'std::system' member_pattern.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PRESENT — CPP_AST_SINKS line 2552 Deserialize has ("", "boost::archive::text_iarchive")
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: Source fix only (std::getenv).

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — CSHARP_AST_SINKS HtmlOutput line 2972 has ("Html","Raw"), ("", "@Html.Raw("), and call_names: ["AppendHtml"]; no Response.Write entry. Pre-M3 vuln.rs get_sinks for (Xss, CSharp) was vec![("Response.Write(",...), ("@Html.Raw(",...), ("Html.Raw(",...), ("AppendHtml(",...)] — Response.Write was the FIRST entry and was lost in the M2 audit.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: CSHARP_AST_SINKS HtmlOutput — add ("Response", "Write") member pattern (or call_names: ["Response.Write"] depending on tree-sitter-c-sharp shape; structured `(receiver,field)` is preferred).

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — CSHARP_AST_SINKS ShellExec line 2950 has ("Process","Start") which structurally requires receiver='Process' — but tree-sitter-c-sharp's invocation_expression for `System.Diagnostics.Process.Start(...)` carries the full multi-segment qualified-name string in extract_call_name. The (receiver, field) split via rfind('.') gives receiver='System.Diagnostics.Process', field='Start' — the existing `("Process", "Start")` does NOT match.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: CSHARP_AST_SINKS ShellExec — add either (a) member_patterns entry ("", "Process.Start") raw-fallback that matches via substring on the call_expression text, OR (b) ("System.Diagnostics.Process", "Start") to handle the qualified form. (a) is more robust and consistent with the M2 convention for multi-segment receivers (see Java's ("", "Runtime.getRuntime().exec")).

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — CSHARP_AST_SINKS FileOpen line 2982 has ("File","Open") but the fixture uses qualified `System.IO.File.Open` — extract_call_name returns the full qualified path; rfind('.') splits as receiver='System.IO.File', field='Open' — does NOT match ("File", "Open").
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: CSHARP_AST_SINKS FileOpen — add raw-fallback entry ("", "System.IO.File.Open") OR ("", "File.Open") raw-fallback. The shorter form ALSO covers the bare ("File","Open") case AND the qualified `System.IO.File.Open` case.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — CSHARP_AST_SINKS Deserialize line 2992 has only ("BinaryFormatter","Deserialize") and ("NetDataContractSerializer","Deserialize"). Pre-M3 get_sinks for (Deser, CSharp) was ["BinaryFormatter.Deserialize(", "NetDataContractSerializer.Deserialize(", "JavaScriptSerializer", "new XmlSerializer", "new SoapFormatter"]. JavaScriptSerializer + new XmlSerializer + new SoapFormatter were all in pre-M3 list and lost.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: CSHARP_AST_SINKS Deserialize — add ("", "JavaScriptSerializer("), ("", "new XmlSerializer"), ("", "new SoapFormatter"). Constructor calls — raw fallback shape.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — ELIXIR_AST_SINKS line 3362 has ("Ecto.Adapters.SQL", "query") but NOT "query!". Elixir convention: `query!` is a separate function (different name in atom-table) that raises on error. tree-sitter-elixir parses `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query!` as a call whose target's identifier-text is exactly 'query!'.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: ELIXIR_AST_SINKS SqlQuery — add ("Ecto.Adapters.SQL", "query!"). Optionally also ("Repo", "query") and ("Repo", "query!") for the Repo-shorthand idiom.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — ELIXIR_AST_SINKS line 3352 ShellExec has only ("System", "cmd"). The Erlang-call shape `:os.cmd` is a SEPARATE syntax (atom-prefixed module call). Pre-M3 get_sinks for (CmdI, Elixir) was ["System.cmd(", ":os.cmd(", "Port.open(", "System.shell("]. Three of four shapes lost.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: ELIXIR_AST_SINKS ShellExec — add ("", ":os.cmd(") raw-fallback for the atom-prefixed Erlang-call shape, plus ("System", "shell") and ("Port", "open") for the missing convention shapes.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — ELIXIR_AST_SINKS line 3377 FileOpen has ("File","read"), ("File","write"), ("Path","join") but NOT ("File", "read!"). Same `!`-suffix convention as the SQL case.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: ELIXIR_AST_SINKS FileOpen — add ("File", "read!"), ("File", "write!"), ("File", "open!"), ("File", "stream!"). Mirrors the existing entries with the bang convention.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — JAVA_AST_SINKS line 2262 FileOpen has ("", "new File(") raw-fallback but the fixture uses the FQN `new java.io.File(p)` whose substring text contains 'java.io.File(' not 'new File('. Substring fallback fires only on exact substring match.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: JAVA_AST_SINKS FileOpen — add ("", "new java.io.File(") raw-fallback OR generalize to ("", ".io.File(") OR add ("", "java.io.File("). Preferred: add the FQN raw-fallback ("", "new java.io.File(") and let tree-sitter-java's parse text carry the full qualified shape.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — JAVA_AST_SINKS line 2298 Deserialize has ("*","readObject") (wildcard receiver) and ("", "ObjectInputStream"). The wildcard `*.readObject` SHOULD fire — but it does not in the empirical run. ROOT CAUSE: the canonical taint engine's flow construction requires the source variable's identifier to reach the sink statement. `d` is the source variable; the sink statement is `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes())).readObject()`. The `d` identifier does appear in the sink statement text, so Phase 5 SSA-active indirect-match should fire. Need to verify experimentally — possibly the AST descent doesn't reach the readObject call from the parameterized chain because the sink site's call-name extraction returns 'readObject' from the OUTER call only, but the source-identifier-bearing sub-expression is buried 3 levels deep. Alternative root cause: the ("*", "readObject") member pattern matches but the `d` variable is not propagated through `d.getBytes()` because that's a method call on d, and the engine's variable-flow tracking may treat `d.getBytes()` as a sanitized/distinct value. EXPERIMENTAL VERIFICATION DEFERRED to M1 of vuln-source-parity-v1.
red_tests_full_audit.candidate_addition: Either (a) add ("*", "readObject") confirmation + verify source flow propagates through `.getBytes()` chained calls, or (b) add ("ObjectInputStream", "readObject") with explicit constructor receiver, or (c) add ("", "new java.io.ObjectInputStream(") raw-fallback. Most likely: an additional source-flow propagation extension is required (taint propagation through `.getBytes()` and `new ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes())` chain) — but to keep this milestone purely additive (source/sink bank only), we add a raw-fallback ("", "new java.io.ObjectInputStream(") which fires on the call_expression text containing the full chain.
red_tests_full_audit.disposition: ADD sink (raw-fallback for the new-expression FQN)

red_tests_full_audit.source_status_in_canonical: PRESENT — TYPESCRIPT_AST_SOURCES (shared by JavaScript via tree-sitter-javascript) line 1791 has ("req","query")
red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PRESENT BUT WRONG TYPE — TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS line 2003 has ("res","send") wired as TaintSinkType::FileWrite (W1-M3 NestJS parity-add). The flow IS detected and emitted as `path_traversal` (vuln_type_from_sink: FileWrite → PathTraversal). Test asserts vuln_type == 'xss'.
red_tests_full_audit.diagnosis: TYPE-MISCLASSIFICATION CASE — sink IS detected, just classified as the wrong VulnType. The pre-M2-audit decision (M2 line 156 'no double-coverage') chose to leave (res,send) wired as FileWrite rather than HtmlOutput. The M2 carry-forward note explicitly said 'M3 vuln_type_from_sink projects FileWrite -> Xss/PathTraversal/etc. via context-aware mapping OR a future reclassification milestone may flip these entries to HtmlOutput; that is out of scope for M2.'
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: Two options. Option A (preferred — mirrors M2 carry-forward note's 'reclassification milestone' framing): MOVE ("res","send"), ("reply","send"), ("response","send") from FileWrite to HtmlOutput sink_type. Option B: ADD a duplicate entry in HtmlOutput while LEAVING the FileWrite entry in place (creates double-coverage, two findings per call site). Option A is correct: `res.send(html_string)` is semantically Xss, NOT PathTraversal. Risk: any current GREEN test that relies on res.send being classified as PathTraversal would flip RED. Mitigation: pre-flight grep for any test that asserts vuln_type=path_traversal on res.send / reply.send / response.send. The pre-existing GREEN test suite (134 tests) does NOT include any such assertion (verified empirically at HEAD: typescript_path_traversal_positive uses fs.readFile not res.send; the only res.send test is the failing javascript_xss_positive itself).
red_tests_full_audit.disposition: RECLASSIFY sink (FileWrite → HtmlOutput for res.send, reply.send, response.send, Response.send)

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PRESENT BUT WRONG TYPE — TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS line 1911 has call_names: ["eval"] wired as TaintSinkType::CodeEval. vuln_type_from_sink: CodeEval → CommandInjection. Test asserts vuln_type == 'deserialization'.
red_tests_full_audit.diagnosis: Test fixture is semantically wrong — `eval(d)` is CodeEval (CommandInjection / RCE), NOT Deserialization. But the test ASSERTS deserialization. Two options: (a) modify the fixture to use `Function(d)()` or `JSON.parse(d)` patterns that legitimately deserialize, or (b) treat eval-on-stringified-data as deser-class.
red_tests_full_audit.alternate_root_cause: Pre-M3 vuln.rs get_sinks for (Deser, JS/TS) was ["JSON.parse(","node-serialize","deserialize(","unserialize("]. The test fixture at vuln_migration_v1 was likely authored against a DIFFERENT canonical taxonomy assumption — the M1 author may have intended `eval(d)` to be re-classified as Deserialization for the JS-specific case where eval is used to revive a JSON payload. This is NOT consistent with the rest of the taxonomy.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: FIXTURE FIX (preferred): change javascript/deserialization_positive.js + typescript/deserialization_positive.ts to use a legitimate Deserialize sink (e.g., `const d = req.query.d; const parsed = require('node-serialize').unserialize(d);`). This is a fixture-edit, not a source/sink-bank edit. Alternative: ADD a node-serialize / unserialize entry to TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS Deserialize bank — but the fixture must ALSO change to exercise that sink.
red_tests_full_audit.disposition: FIXTURE FIX + ADD Deserialize sink (node-serialize unserialize, JSON.parse — the latter is a sanitizer in TS bank, conflict; defer JSON.parse)

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — LUA_AST_SINKS has NO SqlQuery variant. Pre-M3 get_sinks for (SqlInjection, Lua) was ["db:query(", "db:execute(", "conn:execute(", "mysql:query("]. ALL FOUR shapes lost. Lua's `:method` syntax (colon-method-call) is a distinct AST shape from member access (.).
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: LUA_AST_SINKS SqlQuery — add raw-fallback entries ("", ":query("), ("", ":execute(") to catch the colon-method-call form. The wildcard receiver convention for Lua is that the LUA tree-sitter parses `db:query(x)` as a function-call whose function-field is a method_index_expression with operator ':' — extract_call_name_lua returns 'db:query' (with the colon embedded). Raw-fallback substring path matches the call_expression text containing ':query('.

red_tests_full_audit.source_status: PRESENT (Luau shares LUA_AST_SOURCES via get_ast_patterns dispatch)
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: Same as lua_sql_injection_positive — adding to LUA_AST_SINKS auto-covers Luau because Luau dispatches to the same bank.

red_tests_full_audit.fixture_text_summary: let id = Sys.getenv "ID" in Mariadb.Stmt.execute stmt ("SELECT ... " ^ id)
red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — OCAML_AST_SINKS line 3458 SqlQuery has only ("Sqlite3","exec"). Pre-M3 get_sinks for (SqlInjection, OCaml) was ["Mariadb.","Postgresql.","Sqlite3.exec","Mysql."]. Three of four shapes lost.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: OCAML_AST_SINKS SqlQuery — add ("Mariadb.Stmt", "execute"), ("Postgresql", "exec"), ("Mysql", "exec"), ("Sqlite3", "prepare"). The (Mariadb.Stmt, execute) shape uses OCaml's rfind('.') split convention.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — PYTHON_AST_SINKS HtmlOutput line 1714 has call_names: ["Markup", "mark_safe"], member: ("", "|safe"). No `response.write` entry. M4-CF-01 documented this gap explicitly.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: PYTHON_AST_SINKS HtmlOutput — add ("response", "write") and ("Response", "set_data") for Flask Response API.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — RUBY_AST_SINKS has NO SqlQuery variant at all. Pre-M3 get_sinks for (SqlInjection, Ruby) was [".execute(","ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute","#{tainted}".sql_safe,"raw_sql("]. ALL shapes lost.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: RUBY_AST_SINKS — ADD a SqlQuery variant. Patterns: ("*", "execute"), ("", "ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute"), ("", "raw_sql("). The wildcard `("*", "execute")` is broad but mirrors the same-shape entry already in PYTHON_AST_SINKS (cursor.execute). Risk: over-broad — may match Process.execute, Thread.execute, etc. Mitigation: gate to specific receivers if possible: ("connection", "execute"), ("*ActiveRecord*", "execute") via raw-fallback.
red_tests_full_audit.disposition: ADD sink (new SqlQuery bank for Ruby)

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — RUBY_AST_SINKS line 2666 ShellExec has call_names: ["system","exec"] and ("IO","popen"). No backtick-command-literal coverage. tree-sitter-ruby parses `\`#{x}\`` as a `subshell` node — distinct AST shape from call_expression. Pre-M3 get_sinks for (CmdI, Ruby) included `"`"` (backtick char) which fired via substring match on any line containing a backtick.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: RUBY_AST_SINKS ShellExec — there is NO call-shape or member-shape that fits the subshell node. Two options: (a) add a raw-fallback ("", "`") that fires on any descendant whose text contains a backtick — DANGEROUS, would FP-fire on any string containing a backtick; (b) leave as a CARRY-FORWARD EXCEPTION (mirrors the Ruby \bgets\b carry-forward from FAI-v1 — same precedent: 'AST shape that cannot be expressed structurally without false-positive risk'). RECOMMENDED: option (b) — carry-forward documented in plan §5 as PARITY-EXCEPTION-RUBY-BACKTICK.
red_tests_full_audit.disposition: CARRY-FORWARD (AST cannot structurally express without FP risk; mirrors FAI-v1 Ruby \bgets\b precedent)

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PRESENT — RUST_AST_SINKS line 2359 ShellExec has ("", "Command::new") and ("", "std::process::Command")
red_tests_full_audit.diagnosis: Both patterns present. ROOT CAUSE: the source variable `cmd` reaches the sink STATEMENT only via the chained `.arg(&cmd)` — the OUTER call_expression text is `std::process::Command::new("sh").arg("-c").arg(&cmd).status().unwrap()`. The sink-bank match identifies `Command::new` as the sink call. The Phase 5 SSA-active indirect-match needs the source identifier `cmd` to appear in the sink_block's statement-text — which it does (`&cmd`). EXPERIMENTAL VERIFICATION REQUIRED but the empirical run shows zero findings.
red_tests_full_audit.alternate_root_cause: tree-sitter-rust parses the chained call as nested call_expression nodes; the canonical detect_sinks_ast walks all descendants. `Command::new(...)` is the FIRST call in the chain — the descendant matching `("", "Command::new")` is the inner call_expression whose text is `std::process::Command::new("sh")` — does NOT contain `&cmd`. The outer call_expressions (`.arg(...)`, `.status()`) have text containing `cmd` but their function-field text is `arg` or `status` — does NOT match Command::new. The outermost call's text `...arg(&cmd)...` DOES contain cmd but its sink-match path is via call_names: 'unwrap' / member: 'status' / 'arg' — none in ShellExec bank.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: RUST_AST_SINKS ShellExec — add wildcard receiver entry ("*", "arg") with explicit gate? NO — that would be over-broad. Alternative: add a STATEMENT-LEVEL match path so that any SINK at the head of a chained call propagates the chain's source-identifier presence to the sink emission. But that's a TAINT-ENGINE refactor, not a bank addition. ALTERNATIVE B: extend RUST_AST_SOURCES to wrap `std::env::var(...).unwrap()` such that the variable assignment line itself is what flows — already happens. ROOT CAUSE LIKELY: the Rust fixture trips on the `.unwrap()` chain — `let cmd = std::env::var("CMD").unwrap()` — the source-extraction returns the OUTER call (.unwrap) first; extract_first_identifier_arg returns nothing because .unwrap takes no args; the source is silently dropped. Investigate experimentally during M1.
red_tests_full_audit.disposition: INVESTIGATE during M1 — likely needs source-extraction refinement OR sink-pattern broadening (e.g., add ("*", "arg") gated to call chains starting with Command::new)

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — RUST_AST_SINKS line 2376 FileOpen has ("", "File::open") but the fixture uses qualified `std::fs::File::open`. The descendant call_expression for `std::fs::File::open(&p)` has text starting with 'std::fs::File::open' — substring match on `File::open` SHOULD fire (substring). Need to verify whether the source-flow-to-sink path constructs.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: Likely same root cause as rust_command_injection_positive — the .unwrap() wrapping at the source-extraction step. Investigate during M1. If sink-bank addition needed, add ("", "std::fs::File::open") explicit FQN.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — RUST_AST_SINKS line 2393 HttpRequest has ("", "reqwest::get") but NOT the qualified `reqwest::blocking::get`. Substring fallback on `reqwest::get` does NOT match `reqwest::blocking::get`. Pre-M3 get_sinks for (Ssrf, Rust) had ["reqwest::get(", "reqwest::Client", "reqwest::blocking::get(", "ureq::get(", "hyper::Client"]. The blocking variant was lost.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest — add ("", "reqwest::blocking::get"), ("", "reqwest::blocking::Client"), ("", "reqwest::blocking::post").

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — RUST_AST_SINKS line 2409 Deserialize has ("", "serde_json::from_str") — substring match SHOULD fire on `serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>`. Likely same .unwrap() source-extraction issue as the other Rust cases.
red_tests_full_audit.disposition: INVESTIGATE during M1 — likely Rust source-extraction issue affecting ALL 4 Rust RED tests

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — SCALA_AST_SINKS line 3072 FileOpen has ("Source","fromFile") — does NOT match the FQN `scala.io.Source.fromFile`. extract_call_name_scala returns the full qualified path; rfind('.') splits as receiver='scala.io.Source', field='fromFile'.
red_tests_full_audit.missing_addition: SCALA_AST_SINKS FileOpen — add ("", "scala.io.Source.fromFile") raw-fallback OR ("scala.io.Source", "fromFile") qualified-receiver structured.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — SCALA_AST_SINKS line 3083 has ("*","readObject") and ("", "ObjectInputStream("). The FQN `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(` doesn't match `ObjectInputStream(` substring (the new prefix breaks the match? — actually substring match should fire because 'ObjectInputStream(' IS a substring of 'new java.io.ObjectInputStream('). Likely a different root cause — verify experimentally.
red_tests_full_audit.alternate_root_cause: Same Java-style flow propagation issue — `d.getBytes` chained call may not propagate taint through the parameterized constructor chain.
red_tests_full_audit.candidate_addition: Add explicit ("", "new java.io.ObjectInputStream(") raw-fallback entry to ensure deterministic match.

red_tests_full_audit.source_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — SWIFT_AST_SOURCES has NO entry for `request.query[`. M3 added ("", "CommandLine.arguments") but NOT the Vapor request shapes. Pre-M3 get_sources for Swift had [("request.query[", "Vapor query"), ("request.headers.first",...), ("request.body.string",...), ...].
red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: ABSENT — SWIFT_AST_SINKS has NO SqlQuery variant. Pre-M3 had ["sqlite3_exec(","executeQuery(","prepareStatement("]. Only sqlite3_exec retained as call_names.

red_tests_full_audit.sink_status_in_canonical: PARTIAL — SWIFT_AST_SINKS line 2867 ShellExec has ("", "Process()") and ("", "NSTask") and call_names: ["system"]. `Process.launchedProcess` is a static method call; (Process, launchedProcess) tuple — NOT in bank. summary.expected_carry_forward: ruby_command_injection_positive — backtick subshell node not structurally expressible (mirrors FAI-v1 Ruby \bgets\b precedent)

key_findings: Spec framing 'source-bank parity' is partially incorrect — empirical evidence shows sink gaps OUTNUMBER source gaps ~5:1.

Spec language scope (Go/Java/CSharp/Scala/Lua/Elixir × multi vuln types) covers only 12 of 33 RED tests — milestone must EXPAND to all 15 languages with RED tests, or document a 21-test deferred scope.

Six RED tests fall outside the additive-bank-addition pattern: 4 Rust tests (likely source-extraction issue with .unwrap() chain), 2 fixture-design issues (eval-as-deserialization in JS/TS), 1 carry-forward (Ruby backtick), 2 reclassifications (res.send: FileWrite→HtmlOutput).
M2's 'no double-coverage' decision in res.send wiring (kept FileWrite, deferred HtmlOutput as a 'reclassification milestone') comes due now — this is that milestone for that one wiring decision.

Carry-forward sole exception: Ruby backtick subshell. Mirrors the FAI-v1 \bgets\b precedent verbatim — AST shape exists but cannot be structurally distinguished from string-literal backticks without FP risk.
